workflow.critical: The workflow execution engine is governed by: {project-root}/_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml
You MUST have already loaded and processed: {installed_path}/workflow.yaml
Generate the audit report document in {document_output_language}
This workflow produces a single audit report file. Save to output file after each audit step.
This is a TEMPLATE workflow. Initialize report from audit-report-template.md first.

check.ask: Which epic to audit? Provide epic ID (e.g., "qgr", "td", "fra") or "auto" to detect from sprint-status:

check.action: Read {sprint_status} and find the most recently completed epic (status == 'done')

step.action: Set {{output_file}} = "{implementation_artifacts}/reviews/epic-{{epic_id}}/epic-{{epic_id}}-closing-audit-{date}.md"
Create output directory if it does not exist
Read audit-report-template.md and write to {{output_file}} with {{epic_id}} and {{date}} substituted
Load epic stories: read all .md files in {stories_dir}/epic-{{epic_id}}/
From each done/review story, extract: story_key, File List section content, Tasks/Subtasks checklist
Store combined as {{epic_story_data}}

step.critical: This step verifies database schema documentation currency against actual migrations
step.action: List all files in {migration_dir} matching pattern *.cs (exclude Designer.cs and Snapshot.cs)
Parse migration file names — BMAD EF Core migration format: YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_MigrationName.cs
Extract the timestamp from the most recent migration filename
Store as {{latest_migration_date}} (format: YYYY-MM-DD)
Store latest migration name as {{latest_migration_name}}
Read first 15 lines of {database_schema}
Find line matching pattern: "Last Updated:" or "最後更新:" or similar date field
Extract date value as {{schema_last_updated}}
Calculate {{gap_days}} = ({{latest_migration_date}} - {{schema_last_updated}}) in days
Set {{db_audit_severity}} based on gap: - gap > 14 days → CRITICAL - gap 7-14 days → WARNING - gap 0-7 days → OK

Scan {{epic_story_data}} File Lists for new/modified files matching patterns: - Data/Migrations/*.cs (new migrations) - Models/*.cs (new or modified entities) - Data/ApplicationDbContext.cs (DbSet additions)
Store as {{new_entities_in_epic}} list

step.critical: Compare epic file changes against documentation last-updated dates
step.action: Scan {{epic_story_data}} File Lists — extract all modified backend files: - Controllers/**/*.cs - Services/**/*.cs - Models/**/*.cs - Program.cs - Areas/**/*.cs
Categorize by type: Controller, Service, Model, Config
Store as {{modified_source_files}}

For each documentation file in scope, read first 20 lines and find Last Updated date: - {project-root}/docs/project-planning-artifacts/technical-specs/database-schema.md - {project-root}/docs/project-planning-artifacts/architecture/*.md (all files) - {project-root}/docs/project-planning-artifacts/technical-specs/*.md (all files) - {project-root}/docs/project-context.md
For each doc, calculate gap vs most recent epic story completion date
Flag as STALE if gap > 14 days AND doc covers modules changed in this epic
Store as {{stale_docs}} list with: doc_path, last_updated, gap_days, affected_modules
Update {{output_file}} — fill in Section 2 (文檔過時掃描) with {{stale_docs}} findings

step.critical: Identify new patterns in epic that lack Skill coverage
step.action: Read {skills_list} — extract all trigger keywords per skill
Store as {{skill_keyword_map}}: skill_name → [keyword1, keyword2, ...]

Scan {{epic_story_data}} File Lists — collect: - New directories created - New service class names (Services/**/I*.cs, Services/**/*.cs) - New model names (Models/**/*.cs) - New frontend patterns (ClientApp/src/**/*.ts, *.tsx)
Store as {{new_patterns_in_epic}} list

For each item in {{new_patterns_in_epic}}: - Check if any keyword in {{skill_keyword_map}} matches (case-insensitive substring) - If match found → record as covered - If no match → record as uncovered
Calculate {{coverage_pct}} = (covered_count / total_count) * 100
Store {{uncovered_patterns}} = list of patterns with no matching skill
Update {{output_file}} — fill in Section 3 (Skill 覆蓋率分析) with coverage stats and uncovered patterns

step.critical: Identify architectural decisions in epic that lack ADR documentation
step.action: Read {adr_dir}/README.md to get existing ADR list
If README.md not found, list all .md files in {adr_dir}
Store as {{existing_adrs}} list with: adr_id, title, topic keywords

Scan {{epic_story_data}} for architectural decision markers: - New Entity types (new DbSet in ApplicationDbContext.cs) - New Service patterns (new interface + implementation pairs) - New Protocol/Middleware additions (Program.cs changes) - New external integrations (3rd party APIs, new NuGet packages) - New frontend architecture (new store patterns, new context providers)
Store as {{epic_arch_decisions}} list
For each item in {{epic_arch_decisions}}: - Search {{existing_adrs}} for related title/keywords - If match found → mark as COVERED - If no match → mark as NEEDS_ADR
Store {{missing_adrs}} = decisions marked NEEDS_ADR
Update {{output_file}} — fill in Section 4 (ADR 缺口評估) with findings

step.critical: Auto-generate TD story frameworks for all audit findings requiring action
step.action: Collect actionable findings from Steps 1-4: - Step 1: If {{db_audit_severity}} is CRITICAL or WARNING → generate DB Schema update story - Step 2: For each STALE doc → generate doc sync story (group by doc type if multiple) - Step 3: If {{coverage_pct}} < 80% → generate new skill creation story - Step 4: For each missing ADR → generate ADR creation story
Read {sprint_status} and find highest existing td-N-* story number
Set {{next_story_num}} = highest_num + 1

For each actionable finding, create story framework: - Story ID: td-{{next_story_num}}-{descriptive-slug} - Title: 繁體中文描述 - Priority: P0 if CRITICAL, P1 if WARNING/gap, P2 if low coverage - Complexity: S/M/L based on scope - Status: backlog - Acceptance Criteria: 2-4 specific ACs based on finding - Increment {{next_story_num}} for each new story

check.if: one or more fix stories generated
check.action: For EACH generated story: 1. Create story file at: {stories_dir}/epic-td/{{story_id}}.md (Use create-story template format: Story 資訊 table + Story + AC + Tasks sections) 2. Add entry to {sprint_status}: {{story_id}}: backlog # {complexity}, P{priority}, {short_description} (created: audit {{date}}) 3. Create tracking file at: {output_folder}/tracking/active/{{story_id}}.track.md

step.action: In audit report Section 5, add note: "Context Memory DB 整合預留 (AC-9): 若 TD-32a/b/c/d 完成後，以下查詢可改用 DB 介面： - 文檔新鮮度查詢 → doc_index 表 - Skill 觸發歷史 → workflow_executions 表 - 新 Story 寫入 → stories 表"
Update {{output_file}} — fill in Section 5 (修復 Story 生成) with generated story list and file paths
Update {{output_file}} — fill in Section 6 (審計摘要) with overall health score and executive summary
Verify {{output_file}} is complete — all 6 sections filled
Calculate overall audit health score: - DB Schema OK → +25 pts - Docs <= 1 stale → +25 pts - Skill coverage >= 80% → +25 pts - ADR gaps = 0 → +25 pts (Each deduction proportional to severity)
Update audit report Summary section with health score and timestamp
step.output:
## Epic Closing Audit 完成
**Epic:** epic-{{epic_id}} **審計報告:** {{output_file}} **整體健康分數:** {{health_score}}/100
### 建議後續行動
1. 執行已生成的 TD Story 修復任務 2. 若 DB Schema CRITICAL → 優先執行 database-schema.md 更新 3. 若文檔過時 → 依優先級更新 architecture/ 和 technical-specs/ 4. 將審計報告分享給 SM 和架構師審閱
**Tip:** 建議在 Epic 50% 和 75% 進度時也執行一次中期審計。
